title: staphylea japonica f. lanata
intro: Staphylea japonica f. lanata, also known as the Japanese bladdernut, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia. Its leaves, bark, and roots possess medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, boosting the immune system, and acting as an antiseptic to prevent infections. Staphylea japonica f. lanata is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to support overall health and well-being.
